meaning: n.愉快,欢呼v.(使)快活,(对)欢呼
examples: I think he misses her terribly. You might cheer him up.
The colonel was rewarded with a resounding cheer from the men.
A great cheer went up from the crowd.
Sharma gave the crowd something to cheer with a mighty 90 metre swipe over mid-wicket.
It was tennis to stare at, to cheer for, to rewind again and again.
